title: How Well Do You Understand Christian Theology?
description: Thirty questions covering central doctrines, theological terms and differing Christian viewpoints.

questions.question: What does monotheism affirm?
options: No knowledge of God is possible
questions.explanation: Christian theology is monotheistic, confessing one God.

questions.question: What does the Trinity affirm?
questions.explanation: The doctrine confesses one divine being eternally existing as Father, Son and Holy Spirit.

questions.question: What does creation ex nihilo mean?
questions.explanation: The phrase means that God did not shape the universe from pre-existing independent matter.

questions.question: What is providence?
options: God’s sustaining and governing care for creation
A human ability to predict the future
questions.explanation: Providence describes God’s continuing care, preservation and governance of creation.

questions.question: What is the image of God?
options: Humanity’s God-given dignity and vocation to represent him in creation
A status held only by rulers
A quality lost by disabled people
questions.explanation: All human beings bear God’s image and therefore possess inherent dignity and calling.

questions.question: What is sin?
options: Rebellion, corruption and failure to love God and neighbour
questions.explanation: Sin includes distorted desire, action, allegiance and systems opposed to God’s good purposes.

questions.question: What is atonement?
options: The work of Christ that reconciles sinners to God
questions.explanation: Atonement refers to Christ’s saving work, especially through his death and resurrection.

questions.question: What is justification?
options: God declaring sinners righteous through Christ
The believer becoming incapable of sin
questions.explanation: Justification concerns being put right with God by grace through faith.

questions.question: What is sanctification?
options: Growth in holiness through the Spirit
questions.explanation: Sanctification is the Spirit-enabled formation of believers into Christlikeness.

questions.question: What is glorification?
options: The final transformation of believers in resurrection life
questions.explanation: Glorification refers to the completion of salvation in resurrection and renewed creation.

questions.question: What does Christology study?
options: The person and work of Jesus Christ
questions.explanation: Christology examines who Jesus is and what he has accomplished.

questions.question: What does pneumatology study?
questions.explanation: Pneumatology is the theological study of the person and work of the Holy Spirit.

questions.question: What is ecclesiology?
questions.explanation: Ecclesiology considers the church’s identity, practices, mission and leadership.

questions.question: What is eschatology?
options: The theology of last things and Christian hope
questions.explanation: Eschatology concerns resurrection, judgement, Christ’s return and new creation.

questions.question: What is revelation in theology?
options: God making himself and his purposes known
The last book of the Bible only
questions.explanation: Revelation is God’s self-disclosure through creation, history, Scripture and supremely Christ.

questions.question: What does inspiration of Scripture mean?
options: God worked through human authors so Scripture communicates his word
The authors wrote without personalities or contexts
questions.explanation: Inspiration affirms divine agency through genuinely human authors.

questions.question: What does the sufficiency of Scripture mean?
options: Scripture contains everything needed for faithful knowledge of salvation and Christian life
questions.explanation: Sufficiency concerns Scripture’s adequacy for its God-given purpose, not exhaustive information about every subject.

questions.question: What is general revelation?
options: Knowledge of God available through creation and human experience
Secret knowledge given to a few people
Only the teaching of the apostles
questions.explanation: General revelation refers to God’s self-disclosure through creation, conscience and providence.

questions.question: What is special revelation?
options: God’s particular self-disclosure through redemptive acts, Scripture and Christ
questions.explanation: Special revelation refers to God’s more specific saving self-disclosure.

questions.question: What is original sin?
options: The inherited condition and solidarity of humanity in sin
A doctrine that children are personally guilty of every adult act
The belief that matter is evil
questions.explanation: The term describes humanity’s shared fallen condition, though traditions explain its transmission differently.

questions.question: What is substitutionary atonement?
options: Christ acts on behalf of sinners and bears what they could not bear
questions.explanation: Substitution emphasises Christ acting for us in dealing with sin and judgement.

questions.question: What is Christus Victor?
options: An atonement theme emphasising Christ’s victory over sin, death and evil powers
A title for a medieval pope
questions.explanation: Christus Victor highlights liberation through Christ’s triumph over hostile powers.

questions.question: What is adoption in salvation?
options: Believers being welcomed into God’s family
questions.explanation: Adoption describes the gift of belonging to God as his children through Christ.

questions.question: What is union with Christ?
options: Participation in Christ’s life through faith and the Spirit
questions.explanation: Union with Christ gathers together themes of salvation, identity and participation in Christ.

questions.question: What is the kingdom of God?
questions.explanation: The kingdom is God’s rule arriving through Jesus and awaiting future fullness.

questions.question: What is resurrection?
options: The renewal of embodied life by God
The immortality of the soul alone
A metaphor for moral improvement only
The escape of spirits from creation
questions.explanation: Christian hope centres on bodily resurrection, grounded in Jesus’ resurrection.

questions.question: What is annihilationism?
options: The view that the wicked are finally destroyed rather than sustained forever in conscious torment
The belief that death ends everyone without resurrection
questions.explanation: Annihilationism affirms judgement while understanding its final outcome as destruction.

questions.question: What is universalism?
options: The belief that all people will ultimately be saved
The belief in one universal church only
questions.explanation: Universalism teaches that God’s saving purpose will finally include everyone, though forms differ.

questions.question: What is compatibilism?
options: The view that divine sovereignty and meaningful human responsibility are compatible
The belief that all religions teach the same thing
questions.explanation: Compatibilism seeks to affirm both divine determination and genuine responsibility.

questions.question: What is Arminianism?
options: A tradition emphasising prevenient grace, resistible grace and meaningful human response
questions.explanation: Arminian theology affirms salvation by grace while stressing that grace can be resisted.
